notes: Applies ONLY to uniformed paid municipal firefighters who began employment BEFORE July 1, 1996. Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of hypertension or heart disease. Benefits provided in lieu of chapter 568 WC benefits for hypertension/heart disease. Includes retirement/survivor benefits. 'Municipal employer' as defined in § 7-467.

Applies ONLY to regular paid municipal police officers who began employment BEFORE July 1, 1996. Pre-employment exam must have failed to reveal any evidence of hypertension or heart disease. Benefits provided in lieu of chapter 568 WC benefits. Includes retirement/survivor benefits.

condition_specific: Cardiac arrest or myocardial infarction occurring within 24 hours after duty completion
filing_deadline_notes: Employer must contest liability on or before the 28th day after receiving written notice of claim or presumption of compensability is conclusive
notes: Applies to uniformed paid municipal firefighters hired after July 1, 1996. Pre-employment exam by licensed physician must have failed to reveal evidence of condition. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Cardiac arrest or myocardial infarction occurring within 24 hours after duty completion
filing_deadline_notes: Employer must contest liability on or before the 28th day after receiving written notice of claim or presumption of compensability is conclusive
notes: Applies to regular members of paid municipal police departments hired after July 1, 1996. Pre-employment exam by licensed physician must have failed to reveal evidence of condition. Rebuttable by preponderance of evidence.

condition_specific: Hepatitis, meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis, Kahler's Disease (multiple myeloma), non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
notes: Covers uniformed paid and volunteer firefighters. Also includes prostate cancer and testicular cancer under this occupational disease section.

condition_specific: Hepatitis, meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis, Kahler's Disease (multiple myeloma), non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
notes: Covers uniformed members of paid and volunteer fire departments. Also includes prostate cancer and testicular cancer under this occupational disease section.

condition_specific: Hepatitis, meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis, Kahler's Disease (multiple myeloma), non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
notes: Covers regular paid municipal police officers and constables. Also includes prostate cancer and testicular cancer under this occupational disease section.

condition_specific: Hepatitis, meningococcal meningitis, tuberculosis, Kahler's Disease (multiple myeloma), non-Hodgkin's lymphoma
notes: Covers members of volunteer ambulance services. Also includes prostate cancer and testicular cancer under this occupational disease section.

condition_specific: Conditions affecting skin, brain, skeletal, digestive, endocrine, respiratory, lymphatic, reproductive, urinary, or hematological systems
filing_deadline_notes: Surviving dependents may file within 1 year of firefighter's death
notes: Requires 5 years as interior structural firefighter or equivalent fire marshal/investigator. Tobacco exclusion: no cigarette use during 15-year period prior to diagnosis. Benefits offset by other benefits received. If firefighters cancer relief account becomes insolvent, municipal/state employers no longer obligated to provide compensation. Effective October 1, 2023.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress injury meeting American Psychiatric Association diagnostic criteria
notes: Qualifying events include witnessing death, traumatic injury, or COVID-19 exposure. Medical records subject to workers' compensation guidelines.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress injury meeting American Psychiatric Association diagnostic criteria
notes: Qualifying events include witnessing death, traumatic injury, or COVID-19 exposure.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress injury meeting American Psychiatric Association diagnostic criteria
notes: Covers emergency medical services personnel. Qualifying events include witnessing death, traumatic injury, or COVID-19 exposure.

condition_specific: Post-traumatic stress injury meeting American Psychiatric Association diagnostic criteria
notes: Covers Department of Correction employees and telecommunicators. Qualifying events include witnessing death, traumatic injury, or COVID-19 exposure.
